structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections and locked partial-identification tuple are present, and the assumptions/checklist are mostly usable, but the theorem organization is not review-stable: the actual outer-envelope theorem is placed in the proof section, Conjecture 1 is refuted only as prose in Main theorems, and the four-cell witness lacks a fully specified finite program in the theorem statement.

novelty.notes: The current derivation is below the orchestrated flagship floor. The solid contribution is a routine outer-envelope inclusion plus a one-cell counterexample to the proposed cycle criterion; the negative result does not name a specific published estimator/workflow claim that is refuted, and the claimed open-set witness is not yet positioned as a generic-class obstruction against a named target.

correctness.notes: The one-cell Conjecture 1 counterexample is arithmetically credible, but Theorem thm:witness is not proved from displayed data: the local rank-tube constraints, active LP basis, primal optimizer tables, dual residuals, and global-potential implication are asserted rather than constructed or derived.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem thm:witness
perItemFindings.one_line: The theorem asserts an open strict-containment witness without specifying the finite LP constraints, rank-copula tubes, primal optimizer tables, or dual certificate needed to verify the witness.

perItemFindings.label: Proof of Conjecture 2
perItemFindings.one_line: Lines 306-310 assume a witness basis and strictly interior endpoint tables, but these objects are not displayed or derived from the listed marginals.

perItemFindings.label: Cycle-pasting contradiction
perItemFindings.one_line: Lines 328-338 use the key claim that any full-law optimizer induces residuals from one global vertex potential, but this compatibility theorem is neither stated as an assumption nor proved from the LP/KKT system.

perItemFindings.label: Open-set claim
perItemFindings.one_line: Lines 349-353 invoke active-basis stability, but the active basis, constraints, and slacks are not explicit enough to verify continuity or preservation of the claimed residual signs.

perItemFindings.label: Novelty positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: The negative/strict-containment contribution does not identify a specific published estimator, workflow, or claim that relies on the refuted lifted-cellwise shortcut.

perItemFindings.label: Main theorems section
perItemFindings.one_line: The solid outer-envelope theorem is buried under Step-by-step proof, while the Main theorems section foregrounds an unverified witness and states the Conjecture 1 refutation only as prose.

verbatim_critique: The note has the right partial-identification anchor and most required Stage 0 sections, but it is not yet an acceptable flagship Stage 0 derivation. The strongest checked part is the Conjecture 1 counterexample in the Counterexample section: the response-type enumeration gives the advertised sharp interval [4/5,1], and the lifted relaxation can indeed reach the lower value 2/5 by choosing the marginal-envelope vertex with mass 6/10 on the tail-failing cell. That correctly refutes the proposed iff criterion as stated. Theorem thm:witness is the main problem. The displayed four-cell marginals and the residual arithmetic show only that the proposed certificate would have circulation 0.14 if it came from a valid lifted endpoint dual. The proof never gives the local rank-copula tube inequalities, the marginal-envelope inequalities, the active IV/dose constraints, the primal transport tables, or the normalized dual variables. Consequently the statements in the proof that a witness basis exists, that the endpoint tables are strictly interior, and that the residuals are the lexicographically normalized dual residuals are assumptions, not verified consequences of the displayed law. The key strict-containment step also needs a theorem connecting full-law pastability to residuals being exact differences of one global vertex potential. The deterministic telescoping identity is true once exactness is known, but the note does not prove that any full-data optimizer must produce exact residuals on the active graph. The open-set conclusion has the same issue: Robinson-style active-basis stability can only be applied after the finite-dimensional polyhedral system and active basis are fully specified. As written, there is no finite certificate a reader can check. On novelty, the current verified mathematics is below the flagship floor. A one-cell counterexample to a proposed diagnostic and a routine outer-envelope inclusion are useful, but the negative-result rules require a named published target or a generic-class obstruction. The related-work section lists relevant OT and DTE papers, but it does not identify a specific published estimator, workflow, or theorem that uses the refuted lifted-cellwise shortcut. To revise, Stage 0 should either demote the witness to a conjectural certificate, or provide a complete finite LP certificate: explicit observed law P, explicit M, V, and C constraints, primal endpoint tables, dual variables, active basis, slack margins, and a proved lemma that full-law pastability implies exact residuals on the pasting graph. It should also reposition the negative result against a named target or state plainly that the current derivation is a narrower subfield counterexample rather than a flagship theorem.
